Name: NSF SBIR Phase I
Eligibility: US small businesses (<500 employees); must be majority US-owned
Notes: NSF is unique among SBIR agencies — no preliminary data required. Strong emphasis on broader impacts and commercialization potential.

Name: NSF SBIR Phase II
Eligibility: US small businesses with Phase I award
Notes: Phase II builds on Phase I results. Can apply for Phase IIB supplement.

Name: NSF STTR Phase I
Eligibility: US small businesses partnered with a research institution
Notes: Requires formal partnership with a US university or research institution. Research partner must do at least 30% of the work.

Name: NIH SBIR Phase I
Notes: NIH has 24 institutes — target the right one. Study sections matter enormously. Strong preliminary data helps.

Name: NIH SBIR Phase II
Eligibility: US small businesses with Phase I award
Notes: Can request up to $1.75M over 2 years. Commercialization plan is critical.

Name: NIH STTR Phase I
Eligibility: US small businesses partnered with research institution
Notes: Must collaborate with a nonprofit research institution. Research partner does at least 30% of work.

Name: DOE SBIR Phase I
Notes: Topics released annually. Heavily focused on energy efficiency, renewable energy, nuclear, and advanced scientific computing.

Name: DOE SBIR Phase II
Eligibility: US small businesses with Phase I
Notes: Can apply for Phase IIB commercialization supplement up to $1M additional.

Name: DOD SBIR Phase I
Notes: Largest SBIR program. Includes Army, Navy, Air Force, DARPA, MDA, DHA, SOCOM, CBD. Each has own topics and timelines.

Name: DOD SBIR Phase II
Eligibility: US small businesses with Phase I
Notes: Phase III contracts can be sole-source — huge advantage. Build relationships with program managers early.

Name: DARPA SBIR/STTR
Deadline: Multiple cycles per year via DSIP portal
Notes: DARPA topics tend toward breakthrough/high-risk technology. Smaller program than other DOD components but very high prestige.

Name: NASA SBIR Phase I
Notes: Highly specific technical topics aligned with NASA mission needs. Strong technical merit and feasibility required.

Name: NASA SBIR Phase II
Eligibility: US small businesses with Phase I
Notes: Includes potential for Phase III contracts and technology infusion into NASA missions.

Name: USDA SBIR Phase I
Notes: Topics include plant production, animal production, food science, rural development, and forests/related resources.

Name: EPA SBIR Phase I
Notes: Smaller program. Focus on pollution prevention, clean water, air quality monitoring, and waste management technologies.

Name: NIST SBIR
Organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
Notes: Focus on advanced manufacturing, cybersecurity, AI measurement, and standards-related technology.

Name: EDA Build to Scale (B2S)
Notes: Two tracks: Venture Challenge (proof of concept) and Capital Challenge (scaling). For ecosystem builders, not individual startups.

Name: ARPA-E OPEN
Notes: Extremely competitive. Looking for transformational energy technologies. Strong technical team essential.

Name: BARDA DRIVe
Organization: Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority
Eligibility: US entities with health security innovations
Notes: Accelerator model for medical countermeasures. DRIVe program specifically targets non-traditional innovators.

Name: VA Innovation Center (iNET)
Notes: Focus on veteran health outcomes. Includes innovation competitions and partnerships.

Name: CalSEED
Notes: Funded by California ratepayers. Strong focus on equity and disadvantaged communities.

Name: California EPIC Program
Notes: Large-scale program for clean energy innovation. Includes demonstration and deployment funding.

Name: Empire State Development Grants
Notes: Multiple programs including Excelsior Jobs Program, Innovation Hot Spots, and regional economic development grants.

Name: MassCEC Catalyst Program
Notes: Non-dilutive grants for early-stage clean energy companies in Massachusetts.

Name: MassVentures START
Notes: Matching funds for Massachusetts companies that have received federal SBIR/STTR Phase I awards.

Name: CPRIT Product Development
Organization: Cancer Prevention and Research Institute of Texas
Notes: One of the largest state-funded cancer research programs. Requires Texas nexus.

Name: Texas Enterprise Fund
Notes: Deal-closing fund. Requires job creation commitments. Very competitive and politically influenced.

Name: Colorado Advanced Industries Grant
Sector Focus: Advanced Industries (aerospace, bioscience, electronics, energy, IT)
Notes: Two tracks: Proof of Concept and Early-Stage Capital and Retention. Requires matching funds.

Name: Maryland Innovation Initiative (MII)
Notes: Bridge funding between university research and commercialization. Must be affiliated with a Maryland research university.

Name: Ohio Third Frontier
Notes: Multiple programs including Technology Validation, Entrepreneurial Services Provider, and Pre-Seed/Seed.

Name: Virginia Innovation Partnership Authority
Notes: Includes Commonwealth Research Commercialization Fund and other innovation programs.

Name: Georgia Research Alliance
Notes: Venture Development program provides non-dilutive funding and mentorship for Georgia startups.

Name: Google for Startups Cloud Program
Amount: Up to $200,000 in Google Cloud credits
Notes: Not cash — cloud credits. Includes technical support, training, and networking opportunities.

Name: Google for Startups Black Founders Fund
Notes: Cash grant plus cloud credits. Also includes mentorship and Google network access.

Name: Google for Startups Women Founders Fund
Notes: Similar structure to Black Founders Fund but focused on women-led startups.

Name: AWS Activate
Notes: Two tiers: Activate Founders (self-funded, $1K credits) and Activate Portfolio (VC-backed, up to $100K). Not cash.

Name: Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub
Amount: Up to $150,000 in Azure credits + tools
Notes: Free tier available to all. Higher tiers require application. Includes GitHub Enterprise, LinkedIn premium, and mentorship.

Name: NVIDIA Inception
Amount: Hardware discounts, cloud credits, technical support
Notes: Not direct funding — provides GPU discounts, DGX Cloud credits, technical workshops, and go-to-market support.

Name: Stripe Atlas
Amount: Incorporation services + credits ($5K+ in partner perks)
Notes: Not a grant — incorporation service with partner credits from AWS, HubSpot, etc. One-time $500 fee.

Name: HubSpot for Startups
Amount: Up to 90% off HubSpot tools (up to $150,000+ value)
Notes: Requires affiliation with a partner VC, accelerator, or incubator. Deep discounts on CRM and marketing tools.

Name: Salesforce Accelerate
Eligibility: B2B startups building on Salesforce platform
Notes: Best for startups building apps on the Salesforce ecosystem. Includes AppExchange listing support.

Name: Meta Small Business Grants
Eligibility: Small businesses affected by economic challenges
Notes: Typically launched in response to economic events (COVID, natural disasters). Check periodically for open rounds.

Name: Comcast NBCUniversal LIFT Labs
Notes: 12-week accelerator. Takes no equity. Demo day with Comcast leadership. Strong corporate partnership opportunities.

Name: Visa Everywhere Initiative
Notes: Global competition with regional heats. Focus on payments innovation. Good visibility with Visa partnership team.

Name: Mastercard Start Path
Amount: No direct funding but partnership + scale access
Eligibility: Late-seed to Series B fintech/commerce startups
Notes: Not a grant — a partnership accelerator. No equity taken. Access to Mastercard APIs, customers, and network.

Name: Adobe Creative Residency
Notes: One-year residency for creative professionals. Focus on a personal creative project using Adobe tools.

Name: Snap Yellow Accelerator
Notes: Non-dilutive! 16-week accelerator. Strong focus on augmented reality and next-gen social technology.

Name: Knight Foundation Grants
Organization: John S. and James L. Knight Foundation
Eligibility: Nonprofits and for-profits with civic focus
Notes: Focus on informed and engaged communities. Strong interest in local news, civic engagement, and arts.

Name: MacArthur Foundation 100&Change
Notes: Massive single prize. Extremely competitive. Looking for real, measurable, durable solutions to critical problems.

Name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation Grand Challenges
Notes: Grand Challenges Explorations are great entry point — $100K, brief 2-page application. Full Grand Challenges are much larger.

Name: Echoing Green Fellowship
Notes: Highly competitive and prestigious. 18-month fellowship includes leadership development, mentorship, and wellness support.

Name: Ashoka Fellowship
Notes: Must be nominated or apply directly. Multi-stage selection process. Lifetime membership in Ashoka network.

Name: Skoll Award for Social Entrepreneurship
Eligibility: Proven social entrepreneurs with track record
Notes: Not for early-stage. Looking for organizations already demonstrating systems-level change at scale.

Name: Fast Forward Accelerator
Notes: Specifically for technology nonprofits. 5-month accelerator. Strong alumni network in tech-for-good space.

Name: Mozilla Foundation Awards
Notes: Multiple programs including Responsible CS, Data Futures Lab, and Creative Media Awards.

Name: Kauffman Foundation Grants
Notes: Focus on expanding access to entrepreneurship. Primarily funds intermediaries and ecosystem builders, not individual startups.

Name: Omidyar Network Grants
Notes: Impact investment firm + grant-maker. Focuses on responsible tech, reimagining capitalism, and inclusive governance.

Name: XPRIZE Competitions
Notes: Multiple active competitions. Currently includes carbon removal, rainforest, wildfire, feed the next billion. Multi-year timelines.

Name: TechCrunch Disrupt Startup Battlefield
Notes: Massive visibility regardless of winning. Past winners include Dropbox, Mint, Cloudflare. Free exhibition at Disrupt.

Name: Y Combinator
Geographic Focus: Global (must relocate to SF for batch)
Notes: Technically dilutive (7% equity) but included due to outsized value. 3-month program. Interview-driven selection.

Name: MassChallenge
Geographic Focus: Global (programs in US, Israel, Mexico, etc.)
Notes: Zero-equity accelerator. Top startups win cash prizes at Demo Day. One of the largest accelerators globally.

Name: 43North
Eligibility: Startups willing to relocate to Buffalo, NY
Notes: Must commit to operating in Buffalo for 1 year. $1M investment for 5% equity. Includes co-working space and mentorship.

Name: Rice Business Plan Competition
Notes: Largest and richest graduate-level business plan competition. Must have at least one current grad student on team.

Name: MIT $100K Entrepreneurship Competition
Notes: Three tracks: PITCH, ACCELERATE, LAUNCH. Must have at least one MIT-affiliated team member.

Name: Hult Prize
Notes: Campus-level competitions feed into regionals and global finals. Focus on solving pressing social challenges.

Name: Global Social Venture Competition (GSVC)
Organization: UC Berkeley Haas School of Business
Notes: One of the oldest social venture competitions. Strong emphasis on measurable social/environmental impact.

Name: Village Capital
Sector Focus: Agriculture / Education / Energy / Financial Inclusion / Health
Notes: Peer-selection model — cohort members rank each other. Top-ranked companies receive investment.

Name: Arch Grants
Eligibility: Startups willing to locate in St. Louis, MO
Notes: Non-dilutive grant. Must commit to relocating to and operating in St. Louis for at least one year.

Name: FedEx Small Business Grant
Notes: Public voting component. Great marketing opportunity regardless of winning. Social media presence helps.

Name: National Geographic Exploration Grants
Notes: Level I grants ($5K-$10K) are easier to get. Focus on exploration, conservation, and storytelling.

Name: Patagonia Environmental Grants
Notes: Favors smaller, grassroots organizations. Must be focused on root-cause environmental activism.

Name: Johnson & Johnson Innovation JLABS
Amount: Lab space + mentorship (no cash grant; no equity)
Notes: Subsidized lab space at J&J facilities. No equity taken. Access to J&J expertise and potential commercial partnerships.

Name: Breakthrough Energy Fellows
Eligibility: Scientists and engineers with climate innovations
Notes: Focus on technologies with gigaton-scale emissions reduction potential. Fellows get commercialization support and network.

Name: NSF AI Research Institutes
Eligibility: US research institutions (can include industry partners)
Notes: Large-scale multi-institution grants. Individual startups typically participate as partners to a university lead.

Name: DARPA AI Next / AI Forward
Notes: Multiple programs under AI umbrella. Check active BAAs on SAM.gov. Requires understanding of DARPA's mission areas.

Name: IES SBIR
Notes: Focus on evidence-based education technology products. Rigorous evaluation requirements.

Name: Lumina Foundation Grants
Notes: Focus on increasing postsecondary credential attainment, especially for underserved populations.

Name: NIMH SBIR
Notes: Part of NIH SBIR system. Focus on mental health interventions, diagnostics, and digital therapeutics.

Name: SAMHSA Grants
Organization: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
Eligibility: US entities addressing substance abuse / mental health
Notes: Multiple grant programs. Focus on substance abuse prevention, mental health services, and community support.

Name: NEA Grants for Arts Projects
Notes: Cannot fund individual artists directly (must go through organizations). Multiple categories including media arts.

Name: Creative Capital Award
Sector Focus: Arts / Technology / Performing Arts / Visual Arts
Notes: Direct funding to individual artists. Multi-year support model. Strong emphasis on innovative, risk-taking projects.

Name: Google Black Founders Fund (dedicated entry)
Eligibility: Black-founded US startups (Seed to Series A)
Notes: Also includes access to Google mentors, PR support, and technical advisors.

Name: Backstage Capital
Notes: VC fund focused on underrepresented founders. Investments are equity but offers some grant programs periodically.

Name: digitalundivided BIG Program
Notes: Provides data-driven resources, mentorship, and funding support for Black and Latina women entrepreneurs.

Name: SoGal Black Founder Startup Grant
Notes: Includes mentorship from SoGal network in addition to grant funding.

Name: Amber Grant
Notes: Simple application process. Monthly grants plus year-end bonus. Named after Amber Wigdahl who died at 19.

Name: IFundWomen Universal Grant
Notes: Aggregates grants from multiple corporate partners (Visa, Caress, American Express, etc.). Apply once for multiple.

Name: Cartier Women's Initiative
Notes: Three laureates and four finalists per region. Includes mentorship, networking, and media exposure.

Name: Hello Alice Small Business Grants
Notes: Platform aggregating grants from corporate partners. New opportunities posted regularly. Free to apply.

Name: Halcyon Incubator
Notes: 5-month program in DC. Zero equity. Includes housing, workspace, mentorship, and stipend.

Name: Techstars Foundation Grants
Notes: Funds organizations (not individual startups) that support underrepresented founders.

Name: SBIR Road Tour / Startup Events
Notes: Free educational events teaching how to apply for SBIR/STTR. Great starting point for first-time applicants.

Name: Thiel Fellowship
Notes: Must drop out of or skip college. Two-year fellowship with mentorship. Famous alumni include Vitalik Buterin.

Name: Unilever Young Entrepreneurs Award
Notes: Focus on sustainable business solutions. Cash prize plus 12-month mentorship program.

Name: Roddenberry Foundation Catalyst Fund
Notes: Inspired by Star Trek vision of the future. Funds bold, catalytic social change work.

Name: Schmidt Futures
Notes: Multiple programs including Rise, VSOC, and AI2050. Often invitation-based or through partner nominations.

Name: Patrick J. McGovern Foundation
Notes: Focus on responsible use of data and AI for social impact. New foundation (est. 2019) with growing portfolio.

Name: Emerson Collective
Eligibility: Organizations in education, environment, immigration, media
Notes: Often operates through invitation. Mix of grants, investments, and advocacy.

Name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative
Notes: Mix of grants and investments. Science program funds open-source tools and biomedical research.

Name: Ballmer Group Grants
Notes: Focus on children, families, and economic mobility. Primarily funds established nonprofits.

Name: 1517 Fund Grants/Medici Project
Notes: Small grants for early experiments. Also has a VC fund. Focus on builders outside traditional institutions.

Name: Bluemind Foundation (Ocean Tech)
Notes: Niche focus on blue economy and neuroscience of water. Small but growing grant program.

Name: MIT Solve
Notes: Solver teams get funding, mentorship, and MIT network access. Multi-year commitment from MIT Solve community.

Name: Dreamit Ventures (non-dilutive track)
Notes: Corporate-focused accelerator. Some tracks are non-dilutive depending on corporate partner arrangements.

Name: Morgan Stanley Multicultural Innovation Lab
Notes: Technically takes equity but included for diversity focus. 5-month program with Morgan Stanley resources and network.

Name: Cisco Global Problem Solver Challenge
Eligibility: Early-stage tech startups with social impact
Notes: Focus on using technology to solve social/environmental problems. Multiple category prizes.

Name: Wells Fargo Innovation Incubator (IN2)
Eligibility: Clean energy and agriculture tech startups
Notes: Partnership between Wells Fargo and NREL. Provides lab access, technical validation, and commercial pathway support.

Name: Verizon Forward for Good Challenge
Notes: Focus on digital equity and inclusion. Program format varies by year.

Name: Pivotal Ventures (Melinda French Gates)
Eligibility: Organizations advancing women's power and influence
Notes: Focus on women's economic participation and leadership. Mix of grants and investments.

Name: Arnold Ventures
Sector Focus: Criminal Justice / Health / Education / Public Finance
Notes: Strong emphasis on evidence and research. Funds policy-oriented organizations.

Name: IBM Global Entrepreneur Program
Amount: Up to $120,000 in IBM Cloud credits
Notes: Cloud credits and technical mentorship. Access to IBM technology and enterprise channels.

Name: Oracle for Startups
Amount: Up to $100,000 in Oracle Cloud credits
Notes: Cloud credits, technical support, and go-to-market support from Oracle's enterprise sales team.

Name: Dell for Startups
Notes: Hardware discounts and technical advisory services. Works through partner VC network.

Name: Twilio Impact Access Program
Notes: Free or discounted Twilio communication APIs for social impact organizations.

Name: Notion for Startups
Amount: Free Notion Plus for up to 6 months + AI credits
Notes: Part of startup partnerships — typically requires VC or accelerator affiliation.

Name: New York State Energy Research and Development Authority (NYSERDA)
Notes: Multiple programs for clean energy innovation. Includes proof-of-concept, demonstration, and deployment funding.

Name: Pennsylvania Ben Franklin Technology Partners
Notes: Four regional offices across PA. Mix of grants and investments. Strong mentorship network.

Name: Michigan Economic Development Corporation (MEDC)
Notes: Multiple programs including Pre-Seed Fund, Michigan Translational Research and Commercialization.

Name: Hawaii SBIR Matching Grant
Eligibility: Hawaii companies with federal SBIR/STTR awards
Notes: Matching funds for Hawaii companies with SBIR/STTR awards. Easy application process.

Name: New Mexico Small Business Assistance Program
Notes: Free technical assistance from national lab scientists. Not cash — in-kind expert help.

Name: Connecticut Innovations
Notes: State venture fund with grant and investment programs. Strong life sciences focus.

Name: Climer Prize for Early Stage Entrepreneurs
Notes: Non-dilutive prize for Nashville/Tennessee entrepreneurs. Pitch competition format.

Name: DOD Hacking for Defense (H4D)
Amount: No direct funding but validation + DOD access
Notes: Not a grant — connects startups with DOD end users. Valuable for getting first government customer.

Name: In-Q-Tel
Notes: CIA's strategic investment arm. Takes equity but strategic value is immense. Opens door to IC market.

Name: Greentown Labs
Notes: Largest climatetech incubator in North America. No equity taken. Lab space + corporate partner access.

Name: Clean Energy Trust
Notes: Focus on Great Lakes/Midwest region. Mix of grants and impact investments.

Name: USAID Development Innovation Ventures (DIV)
Notes: Three stages of funding. Open to anyone — individuals, companies, nonprofits. Solutions must target developing countries.

Name: DoD Rapid Innovation Fund (RIF)
Eligibility: US small businesses with defense technology
Notes: Bridges gap between prototype and fielded capability. Requires clear DOD transition partner.

Name: New Enterprise Allowance (NEA) / SBA Emerging Leaders
Notes: Not a cash grant. Free intensive business training and mentorship through SBA district offices.

Name: Activate Fellowship
Notes: Two-year embedded fellowship at a national lab or research university. Salary + research funding + entrepreneurship training.

Name: Cyclotron Road / Activate Berkeley
Notes: Embedded at Lawrence Berkeley National Lab. Two-year program to de-risk hard-tech innovations.

Name: New Voices Foundation Grants
Notes: Focuses on women of color founders. Includes business support and community building.

Name: Sephora Accelerate
Amount: Mentorship + retail placement (no equity early program)
Notes: 6-month brand-building program. Potential for Sephora retail placement. Focus on BIPOC-owned beauty brands.

Name: Target Accelerators
Amount: No direct funding but Target retail access
Notes: Access to Target stores, customers, and supply chain. Multiple cohort tracks.

Name: Samsung NEXT
Notes: Mix of investments and non-dilutive support. Provides access to Samsung technology and distribution.

Name: Kapor Capital (gap-closing portfolio)
Eligibility: Founders closing gaps of access for underserved communities
Notes: Technically VC but included for strong mission alignment. Focus on gap-closing startups.

Name: NIST Manufacturing Extension Partnership (MEP)
Notes: Network of centers providing technical assistance to manufacturers. Subsidized consulting and training.

Name: IndieBio Accelerator
Notes: World's largest biotech accelerator. Takes equity but included for biotech relevance. 4-month program with lab space.

Name: Camelback Ventures Fellowship
Notes: Focus on entrepreneurs of color working in education and workforce development.

Name: Stand Together Foundation
Notes: Catalyst program provides grants + mentorship. Focus on community-driven solutions, not top-down policy.

Name: Resolution Project Social Venture Challenge
Notes: Campus-level pitch competitions. Small seed grants but strong mentorship network for young social entrepreneurs.

Name: Miller Center GSBI
Eligibility: Social enterprises scaling in developing markets
Notes: Executive-education style accelerator for social enterprises. Silicon Valley mentors + online curriculum.

Name: Surdna Foundation
Eligibility: Nonprofits in sustainable environments, thriving cultures, just economies
Notes: Focus on racial and social justice through sustainable environments, thriving cultures, and inclusive economies.

Name: Barr Foundation
Eligibility: Organizations in climate, education, arts (primarily Boston)
Notes: One of the largest foundations in New England. Strong focus on climate and education in Boston metro area.

Name: Draper Richards Kaplan Foundation
Notes: Very prestigious. DRK partner joins your board. $300K over 3 years. Looking for proven founders ready to scale.

Name: Skoll Foundation (Community Grants)
Notes: In addition to the Skoll Award, offers investment in social entrepreneurs through multiple programs.

Name: Civic Innovation Challenge (NSF + DHS)
Notes: Two-stage competition. Requires partnership between community org and research institution.

Name: DOT SBIR
Notes: Focus on transportation safety, infrastructure, and emerging mobility solutions.

Name: DHS SBIR
Notes: Topics include cybersecurity, border security, disaster resilience, and chemical/biological defense.
